title: 10 best herbal decoctions for upset stomach
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for upset stomach are a natural remedy that can provide relief from digestive discomfort, nausea, and bloating. These decoctions are made by steeping herbs in hot water to release their active compounds, which then help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. Examples of herbal decoctions that can aid in alleviating upset stomach include peppermint tea, ginger infusion, and chamomile broth. By sipping on these soothing concoctions, individuals can find relief from symptoms and improve their overall quality of life, allowing them to enjoy daily activities without discomfort or distraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its natural ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion. The herb's bitter properties help to increase saliva production, which aids in breaking down food more efficiently. Additionally, yellow gentian decoctions can help to soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by upset stomachs. As a result, this herbal remedy can provide fast relief from bloating, cramps, and nausea associated with indigestion and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_properties: Bitter principles: Yellow gentian contains bitter compounds that stimulate digestive secretions, increasing the flow of bile and digestive enzymes to relieve indigestion and alleviate bloating.
Alkaloids: The alkaloid content in yellow gentian has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract and alleviating symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and cramps.

Tannins: Tannins present in yellow gentian decoctions have astringent properties, which can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the stomach, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_constituents: Amarogentin: Amarogentin, an iridoid glycoside, has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and calm the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.

Lutein: Lutein, a carotenoid pigment, has antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing of the stomach mucosa, thus alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.

Gentianine: Gentianine, an iridoid glycoside, has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the stomach muscles, reduce spasms, and alleviate cramping, making it easier to manage symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary part used due to their high content of bitter compounds and alkaloids, which have a soothing effect on the stomach.
Leaves: Leaves are another part used, as they contain similar compounds to the roots, providing relief from stomach upset and indigestion.
Buds: Buds are used in decoctions for their mild bitter properties, which help to stimulate digestion and alleviate stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of dried gentiana lutea roots in the fall season when the plant is mature.
Chop the dried gentiana lutea roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Steep 1 tablespoon of chopped gentiana lutea roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their bioactive compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to separate the solids from the liquid.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze it for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with upset stomach because it contains compounds that soothe and calm the digestive system. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines. Additionally, licorice flavonoids have been shown to stimulate the production of mucin, a thick protective lining in the gut that helps to prevent damage from digestive acids and enzymes. This results in a calming effect on the stomach, reducing symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and heartburn associated with upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Flavonoids: Licorice contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, leading to improved digestion and reduced discomfort.

Licoricidin: This compound has anti-spasmodic properties, which helps to relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing cramps, spasms, and other symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and acid reflux.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to soothe and calm the stomach lining by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of stomach acid, thereby alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.

Flavonoids: Contribute to the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra, helping to protect the stomach lining from damage and promote healing of ulcers and other gastrointestinal issues.

Triterpenoids (specifically 18α-Glycyrrhetinic acid): Have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines, and may also help to regulate the body's response to stress, which can contribute to upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are the most used part for upset stomach as they contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe the stomach.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are also used for upset stomach as they are believed to have a calming effect on the digestive system.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used for upset stomach as they contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-4 parts of dried roots in late summer or early fall for optimal quality.
Chop the dried roots into smaller pieces using a sharp knife or mortar and pestle.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of the chopped roots with 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 5-10 minutes or simmer for 15-20 minutes for a stronger decoction.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with upset stomach because it stimulates digestion, reducing inflammation and spasms in the gastrointestinal tract. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger also help to soothe the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as bloating and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's natural antimicrobial effects can help to eliminate harmful bacteria and other microorganisms that may be contributing to an upset stomach. By promoting a healthy gut environment, herbal ginger decoctions can provide fast and effective relief for digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Digestive Enzyme Inhibition: Ginger's active compound, gingerol, inhibits the production of digestive enzymes that break down food too quickly, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with upset stomach.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Activity: Ginger's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as cramping, bloating, and discomfort.

Gastric Motility Regulation: Ginger's bioactive compounds, particularly gingerol and shogaol, regulate gastric motility by increasing the movement of food through the digestive system, reducing symptoms like constipation and indigestion.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help alleviate nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the vomiting center in the brain and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thus relieving symptoms of upset stomach, such as nausea and vomiting.

Zingibain: This proteolytic enzyme has anti-inflammatory properties and helps break down proteins that can cause digestive discomfort, making it easier for the body to digest food and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used to make decoctions for an upset stomach because they contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and digestive properties.
Roots: Roots are used to make decoctions for an upset stomach because they contain similar compounds to rhyzomes, which help to soothe the digestive system and reduce nausea.
Barks: Barks are used to make decoctions for an upset stomach because they contain bioactive compounds that can help to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Select a sufficient quantity of fresh or dried zingiber officinale rhizomes weighing approximately 30 grams.
Rinse the rhizomes under running water and pat them dry with a clean cloth for 2 minutes.
Combine the rinsed zingiber officinale rhizomes with 1.5 liters of boiling distilled water in a saucepan.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture infuse for 10 to 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with upset stomach because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The gel of the aloe leaf contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to relax the muscles in the gut, alleviate cramps, and ease spasms. Additionally, aloe decoctions can help restore the natural balance of gut flora, promoting a healthy digestive environment and alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as bloating, nausea, and diarrhea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach, including cramps, bloating, and diarrhea.

Soothing and protective mucilage: The gel-like substance found in aloe vera contains mucilages that form a soothing film on the gut lining, protecting it from irritation and damage caused by acid reflux, digestive enzymes, or other irritants, which can help calm an upset stomach.

Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants, such as vitamins C and E, help neutralize free radicals in the body that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract, further exacerbating an upset stomach.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: Help soothe and calm the stomach lining by promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria and reducing inflammation.
Aloin: Act as a natural laxative to help regulate bowel movements and alleviate constipation, which can contribute to upset stomach.
Anthraquinones: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce irritation and inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach such as bloating and cramping.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their gel-like interior is used to make decoctions due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that can calm stomach discomfort.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome's extract is used in decoctions as it has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate stomach cramps and discomfort.
Roots: Aloe vera's roots are used in decoctions for their potential to aid digestion and reduce stomach inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100-200 grams of aloe vera leaves and 500 milliliters of distilled water for the decoction.
Wash the aloe vera leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Slice the aloe vera leaves into thin pieces and add them to a heat-resistant container.
Heat the distilled water to a temperature of 95-100 degrees celsius in 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the aloe vera solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's mucilages help to calm irritated linings in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, fenugreek's fiber content can help to regulate bowel movements and prevent diarrhea or constipation. As a result, drinking fenugreek decoctions can provide relief from symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and stomach cramps, allowing for a smoother digestion process and overall sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fenugreek contains anti-inflammatory compounds like galactomannans, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach such as bloating and cramps.

Mucilage content: Fenugreek seeds contain a type of mucilage called galactose-rich polysaccharides, which forms a protective barrier in the stomach, soothing irritations, and reducing inflammation, making it an effective natural anti-inflammatory agent for stomach upset.

Antioxidant properties: Fenugreek's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage the gut lining and cause digestive issues, promoting overall gut health and reducing symptoms of upset stomach such as nausea and diarrhea.
remedy_constituents: Trigonelline: It helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as pain and discomfort.
4-Hydroxyisoleucine: This compound has been shown to slow down gastric emptying, which can help to reduce symptoms of acid reflux and other upset stomach issues.
Saponins: Saponins in fenugreek decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain mucilages, which help soothe and calm the digestive system.
Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing stomach inflammation and discomfort.
Roots: They contain fiber and other compounds that help regulate digestion and alleviate upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of dried trigonella foenum graecum leaves and flowers in the morning.
Crush the harvested trigonella foenum graecum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Boil 1 liter of water in a pot and then add the crushed trigonella foenum graecum powder.
Reduce the heat and simmer the mixture for 5-10 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with upset stomach because its soothing properties calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and spasms. The anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, gently ease cramping and discomfort, while its antibacterial properties help eliminate any underlying infections that may be contributing to the issue. As a natural relaxant, chamomile also reduces stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate stomach upset, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation that benefits digestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach like bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic compounds in chamomile, including flavonoids and terpenes, relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can exacerbate upset stomach symptoms.

Soothing mucous membrane property: Chamomile's soothing compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation of the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, which can help calm symptoms like heartburn, nausea, and vomiting.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with upset stomach by reducing inflammation and spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, promoting relaxation and ease of digestion.

Matricaria chamomilla flavonoids (e.g., apigenin, luteolin): These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the stomach lining and reduce irritation, thereby alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.
α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which help calm the stomach and reduce inflammation, making it easier to manage upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm digestive issues.
Leaves: Used for their ability to provide relief from digestive discomfort and inflammation.
Seeds: Used for their carminative properties, which can help reduce gas and alleviate stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and sift them to remove any lumps.
Combine the sifted flowers with 1 cup of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Discard the solids and add honey to taste if desired to sweeten the decoction.
Store the cooled decoction in a glass container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its soothing and calming properties. The volatile oils present in lemon balm, such as citral and geraniol, have a gentle relaxing effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and inflammation that can cause discomfort and pain. Additionally, lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to ease cramps and contractions in the stomach, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. As a result, lemon balm decoctions can provide relief from symptoms such as bloating, indigestion, and nausea associated with upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm decoctions contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as bloating and cramping.

Antispasmodic effects: The volatile oils present in lemon balm decoctions have antispasmodic properties that can help relax the smooth muscles of the stomach, reducing spasms and cramps associated with indigestion and upset stomach.

Carminative properties: Lemon balm decoctions contain compounds that can help eliminate gas and bloating from the digestive system by stimulating digestion and improving intestinal motility, which can provide relief from symptoms such as discomfort and flatulence.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic acid, rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Menthol: A terpene, menthol has a calming effect on the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and cramps associated with upset stomach.
Luteolin: A flavonoid, luteolin has anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties that help reduce inflammation and cramping in the stomach, promoting relaxation and ease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly citral, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Stems: They are sometimes used, as they contain a similar composition to leaves and can provide a similar effect when made into decoctions.
Roots: They can be used, although less commonly, as they are believed to have a more sedative effect compared to the leaves and stems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Combine melissa officinalis leaves and flowers with water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator and use it within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley decoctions helps with upset stomach because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, volatile oils, and other bioactive compounds that work together to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The anti-inflammatory properties of parsley help reduce inflammation in the gut, while its carminative effects relieve gas and bloating. Additionally, parsley's ability to stimulate digestion and improve bowel function helps to restore balance to the digestive system, providing rapid relief from symptoms such as cramps, diarrhea, and nausea associated with upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Carminative and antispasmodic properties: Parsley helps to reduce muscle spasms in the digestive tract, relieving cramps and bloating associated with upset stomachs.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Parsley's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds soothe irritated tissues and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can exacerbate stomach issues.
Digestive enzyme stimulant properties: Parsley contains enzymes that help stimulate digestion, improving nutrient absorption and reducing symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and cramps.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that helps soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
Ligustilide: A sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, helping to reduce bloating, cramps, and gas in the stomach.
Carvone: A monoterpene that has carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate nausea, vomiting, and stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their carminative properties, which help relieve gas, bloating, and cramps associated with an upset stomach.
Stems: The stems are often used because they contain compounds that can help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
Roots: The roots are sometimes used in decoctions to provide relief from digestive issues, such as nausea and indigestion, due to their antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petroselinum crispum leaves in the morning for maximum potency and minimal moisture content.
Rinse the leaves with cold water to remove dirt and debris for 2 minutes.
Chop the leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife for 5 minutes to increase surface area.
Steep 1 teaspoon of chopped petroselinum crispum in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove solids immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its soothing properties, which calm inflammation in the digestive tract. The decoction's caraway seeds contain a unique compound called cuminaldehyde, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. This combination helps to ease symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with an upset stomach. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles, herbal caraway decoctions promote a sense of relief and aid in the recovery from digestive distress.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Caraway decoction helps relax the muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause abdominal pain and discomfort.

Carminative property: Caraway's volatile oils, such as limonene and beta-pinene, help to release gas bubbles in the stomach and intestines, alleviating bloating, flatulence, and discomfort associated with indigestion.

Antimicrobial property: Caraway's antimicrobial compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, such as Helicobacter pylori, which can contribute to gastritis, ulcers, and other digestive disorders.
remedy_constituents: Carvone: A terpene with carminative properties, helping to alleviate stomach discomfort by reducing gas and inflammation in the digestive tract.
Ligustilide: A sesquiterpene lactone with anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic effects, which help to soothe stomach muscles and reduce nausea and cramping.

Flavonoids (specifically, apigenin and luteolin): A class of phenolic compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the stomach lining from damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Carum carvi are commonly used to make decoctions due to their carvone content, which has a calming effect on the digestive system.
Roots: The roots of Carum carvi are used in decoctions to help alleviate stomach pain and discomfort, as they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Carum carvi are used to make decoctions that aid in digestion and relieve stomach upset, possibly due to their content of carminative compounds that help ease gas and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest carum carvi seeds from mature plants by cutting them when they are completely dry.
Measure 1 teaspoon of carum carvi seeds to use as the decoction ingredient.
Combine the measured carum carvi seeds with 1 cup of boiling water and let it steep for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the decoction to remove the seeds by pouring it through a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Allow the decoction to cool down before serving or storing it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its soothing properties that calm digestive issues. The roots of the burdock plant contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that feeds good bacteria in the gut, promoting a balanced gut microbiome. This leads to reduced inflammation and improved digestion, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Additionally, burdock's anti-inflammatory compounds help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinones: Burdock root contains anthraquinones, which have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to soothe and reduce inflammation in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as cramps and diarrhea.

Inulin: Inulin, a type of fiber found in burdock root, helps to regulate bowel movements, prevent constipation, and support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can help to restore balance to an upset stomach.

Phenolic acids: Burdock root is rich in phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms of upset stomach like bloating and nausea.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin is a type of fructan that acts as a prebiotic, promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which helps alleviate digestive issues and soothe an upset stomach.
Saponins: Saponins in Arctium lappa have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and relieving symptoms of upset stomach, such as bloating and discomfort.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Arctium lappa decoctions have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, protecting the gut lining from damage and infection, which helps to calm an upset stomach and promote digestive health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Arctium lappa are used to make decoctions as they contain inulin, a prebiotic that can help soothe and calm the digestive system.
Stems: The stems of Arctium lappa are used to make decoctions as they contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can help coat the stomach and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of Arctium lappa are used to make decoctions as they contain antioxidants and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried arctium lappa root and 4 cups of water in a saucepan.
Bring the water to a boil then reduce heat to a simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried arctium lappa root to the cooled liquid and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the cooled and strained decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with upset stomach because they contain bioactive compounds that possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The roots and leaves of the dandelion plant have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, including bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. When consumed as a decoction, these compounds help to calm the gut lining, reduce inflammation, and restore the natural balance of gut bacteria, providing relief from stomach discomfort and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Bitter compounds: Dandelion contains bitter compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, which stimulate digestive enzymes and increase bile secretion, helping to alleviate indigestion, bloating, and nausea.
Inulin: Inulin, a type of fiber found in dandelion root, acts as a prebiotic, promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, regulating bowel movements, and soothing stomach irritation.

Choleretic properties: Dandelion's choleretic properties help stimulate the production of bile, which aids digestion, relieves symptoms of indigestion, and reduces inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are commonly used to make teas and decoctions due to their bitter compounds, which help stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
Buds: The buds of the dandelion plant contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help soothe digestive issues such as bloating and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale root material or two teaspoons of fresh material.
Combine the herb material with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid using a piece of cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Take one-half to one cup of the decoction three times daily after meals and at bedtime.
Store any leftover decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to two days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its soothing properties, which calm digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort. The natural compounds present in grapes, like flavonoids and polyphenols, have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce irritation in the digestive tract, allowing the gut to heal and recover more quickly. Additionally, grape decoctions can help regulate bowel movements and ease symptoms of diarrhea or constipation, providing relief from an upset stomach and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, soothing symptoms of an upset stomach such as bloating and cramps.

Polyphenols: The polyphenolic compounds present in grapes have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause gut issues, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and alleviating symptoms of indigestion and nausea.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve stomach cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to soothe and calm digestive issues, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach.
Barks: Containing tannins and other compounds, grape barks have natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help stop diarrhea and alleviate stomach upset.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried vitis vinifera leaves and stems in early morning.
Rinse the herbs with filtered water to remove dirt and debris then chop.
Measure one teaspoon of chopped herb for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the herb mixture in boiling water for 10-15 minutes then strain.
Store the cooled decoction in airtight containers for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The active compound curcumin, found in high concentrations in turmeric roots, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that reduce inflammation and irritation in the gut, easing symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Additionally, turmeric's natural antimicrobial properties help eliminate harmful bacteria and fungi, promoting a healthy balance of gut flora and alleviating upset stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Turmeric contains curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in turmeric decoction can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the gut, which can contribute to upset stomach and other gastrointestinal issues.

Digestive enzyme stimulant properties: Turmeric has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, such as amylase and lipase, which can help break down food properly and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and gas.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm digestive issues, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal decoctions for upset stomach.
Roots: Known for their ability to reduce inflammation and ease symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), turmeric roots are a popular choice for herbal remedies targeting digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried berberis aristata root powder into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Add one cup of boiling water to the cup or teapot and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup or container.
Discard the solids and add one tablespoon of honey to the decoction if desired for taste.
Drink the entire decoction immediately after preparation, ideally within 15-20 minutes for maximum benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle decoctions helps with upset stomach because it has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues such as indigestion, nausea, and bloating. The silymarin compounds present in milk thistle have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders. Additionally, its bitter flavor stimulates digestion and helps to break down food more efficiently, reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of relief.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: A bioflavonoid that protects the liver and helps repair damaged cells, reducing inflammation and soothing digestive issues caused by irritation or infection.
Flavonoids and polyphenols: Antioxidant compounds that reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and damage to gut lining, which can contribute to stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea.

Sterols and saponins: Compounds that help regulate digestion, reduce spasms, and soothe the digestive tract, easing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and other digestive disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of milk thistle contain silymarin, a flavonoid that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds are rich in oil and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm digestive issues such as upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried silybum marianum seeds per eight ounces of water.
Bring the water to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan.
Add the measured silybum marianum seeds and reduce heat to a simmer for 15-20 minutes.
